steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence introduces Portugal's voting age (18), then pivots to Argentina's voting age (16). The blank must connect two facts that directly oppose each other.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘By contrast,’: signals opposition between two different facts. Portugal's higher age vs. Argentina's lower age is a direct contrast. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘For example,’: would mean Argentina illustrates Portugal's rule, but Argentina's age is different from Portugal's, not an example of it. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Firstly,’: implies a sequence or list, but the sentence is not listing steps. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Therefore,’: signals a logical consequence, but Argentina's voting age does not follow from Portugal's. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
